sglang.title: Support NextN (MTP) speculative decoding for DeepSeek-V3/R1
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
We implemented NextN (MTP) speculative decoding for DeepSeek-V3/R1 based on EAGLE 2 on Triton backend (#3466) and achieved 1.76x speed up with CUDA Graph and Torch.compile compatibility. In current benchmark, we achieved 77 token/s output throughput on batch size 1.
In our implementation,

vllm.title: [WIP] TPU V1 Support Refactored
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is a rebase and modification of @robertgshaw2-redhat original PR for TPU support in vLLM V1 from 2 months ago #10241
Currently, TPU attention kernel has no support for mixing prefills and decodes in the same scheduler iteration. As a result, this PR separates the requests to (1) prefills and

vllm.title: [Frontend] Optionally remove memory buffer used for uploading to URLs in run_batch
vllm.author: pooyadavoodi
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We also retry a few times in case an exception (e.g. timeout) happens during the upload.

Fix missing parentheses
Before

After

Sequence(seq_id=0, status=RUNNING, num_blocks=78)

vllm.title: [Misc] Log time consumption of sleep and wake-up
vllm.author: waltforme
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The sleep mode is a nice feature!
To better understand how performant the feature is, we want to have some numeric readouts. Logging the time to fall asleep and wake up may be a good start, so that's all about this PR.

vllm.title: [VLM] Keep track of whether prompt replacements have been applied
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR updates _apply_hf_processor_main and _cached_apply_hf_processor in BaseMultiModalProcessor to explicitly return whether prompt replacements have been applied, so that we don't have to guess by searching for prompt replacements in the processed prompt. This should avoid issues like #13155 (co

vllm.title: [V1] Simplify GPUModelRunner._update_states check
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
I had actually independently discovered and fixed the bug also fixed by just-merged PR #13126 and was just opening a PR when I saw it!
I thought it would be simpler though to check removed_req_indices instead since this covers requests that are removed for any reason (i.e. both unscheduled and finis

vllm.author: zhouyu5

It fixes the error on Gaudi when enabling guided decoding:
RuntimeError: synNodeCreateWithId failed for node: strided_insert with synStatus 1 [Invalid argument]
After fix, all UT have passed for guided decoding and tool calling, including:
vllm-fork/tests/entrypoints/llm/test_guided_generate.py
vllm

vllm.title: [Quant][Perf] Use moe_wna16 kernel by default for MoEs with many experts
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
For GPTQMarlin and AWQMarlin it seems the moe_wna16 kernel is faster for experts with dozens of experts, based on testing Qwen/Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B-Chat-GPTQ-Int4 (60 experts), TechxGenus/DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite-Instruct-AWQ (64 experts), and cognitivecomputations/DeepSeek-R1-AWQ (256 experts)
cc @Eliza

vllm.title: [Core] Reduce TTFT with concurrent partial prefills
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Replaces #10061, as inspired by @njhill and @comaniac's comments. Co-authored by @prashantgupta24
Context: our customers running large multi-tenanted SaaS deployments of vLLM have a problem where high volumes of small-prompt requests are usually processed smoothly, but quickly pile up in a giant que

vllm.title: [BugFix] Don't scan entire cache dir when loading model
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Recent PR #12926 added logging of the time to load model weights, but to determine whether any new files were downloaded to the cache, scans the entire local HF cache dir before and after.
This can be very expensive if there is a large cache, and/or it's a remote filesystem mount which is common.
Wi

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix search start_index of stop_checker
test script
For example
output_text="abcdef", new_char="cdef", stop_str="de", start_index should be 1.
